title: Crispy Coconut Korean Tofu Skillet
description: A vegan-friendly Korean dish featuring crispy tofu, desiccated coconut, and a cheesy pull, all cooked to perfection in an iron teppan skillet.
instructions: Heat the iron teppan skillet over medium-high heat.
In a shallow dish, mix together the cornstarch, korean chili flakes, and a pinch of salt.
Toss the tofu cubes in the cornstarch mixture to coat.
Add the vegetable oil to the preheated skillet and double-fry the tofu until crispy and golden brown.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, and maple syrup.
Pour the sauce into the skillet and bring to a simmer.
Add the desiccated coconut to the skillet and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the coconut is toasted and the sauce has thickened.
Serve the crispy tofu and coconut mixture hot, garnished with chopped green onions and sesame seeds.
